job_description:
About the job
Responsibilities

Kforce has a client in Altamonte Springs, FL that is seeking a Data Engineer. Responsibilities:

 Develop, maintain, and optimize Azure Data Factory (ADF) pipelines
 Work with Snowflake for data loading, transformation, and performance tuning
 Support maintenance processes across the data environment, including debugging and enhancing existing pipelines
 Manage and assist with code migrations and integration tasks
 Work with APIs, data connections, data flows, and relational databases to ensure smooth data movement
 Troubleshoot issues and improve system reliability
 Communicate with business partners to understand consumer expectations and data needs
 Support light data science tasks as needed
 Contribute to team and platform growth as the role expands

Requirements

 Bachelor's degree (required)
 Strong hands-on experience with Azure Data Factory (ADF)
 Proficiency in Snowflake and SQL
 Solid understanding of APIs, data flows, and relational database structures

Preferred Qualifications

 Master's degree preferred
 Certifications in ADF, Snowflake, Azure, or related technologies
 Experience with consumer related strategy or customer analytics is a plus
 Experience collaborating with product management teams

The pay range is the lowest to highest compensation we reasonably in good faith believe we would pay at posting for this role. We may ultimately pay more or less than this range. Employee pay is based on factors like relevant education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, seniority, location, performance, union contract and business needs. This range may be modified in the future.

We offer comprehensive benefits including medical/dental/vision insurance, HSA, FSA, 401(k), and life, disability & ADD insurance to eligible employees. Salaried personnel receive paid time off. Hourly employees are not eligible for paid time off unless required by law. Hourly employees on a Service Contract Act project are eligible for paid sick leave.

Note: Pay is not considered compensation until it is earned, vested and determinable. The amount and availability of any compensation remains in Kforce's sole discretion unless and until paid and may be modified in its discretion consistent with the law.

This job is not eligible for bonuses, incentives or commissions.

Kforce is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, protected veteran status, or disability status.

By clicking “Apply Today” you agree to receive calls, AI-generated calls, text messages or emails from Kforce and its affiliates, and service providers. Note that if you choose to communicate with Kforce via text messaging the frequency may vary, and message and data rates may apply. Carriers are not liable for delayed or undelivered messages. You will always have the right to cease communicating via text by using key words such as STOP.

About the job
Title: Data Engineer (Tampa)

Company: Peoples Gas System

State and City: Florida - Tampa

Shift: 8 Hr. X 5 Days

WHO WE ARE? 

Peoples Gas System is the fastest growing natural gas utility in the Southeast with career opportunities that fit your future. Our organization is diverse – in people, opportunities, and careers. We foster a dynamic and inclusive workplace where safety, innovation and collaboration are at the heart of everything we do. 

HOW YOU’LL HELP DRIVE THE FUTURE OF NATURAL GAS? 

The Data Engineer – PGS is responsible for the creation, maintenance, and support the complex computer database systems and data models. Plan, coordinate, and implement schedule updates to the production Business Development data models. Develop and support data sources, data models, connections, ingestion and utilization. Work with development teams, affiliates, business owners and other stakeholders to design, develop and deliver database solutions to the business to enable data migration, governance and management of the organization’s data, analytics, predictive modeling and decision-making capabilities. With minimal oversight, independently performs technically complex assignments within established policies and procedures and in compliance with established company standards.

WHAT YOU NEED TO SUCCEED? 

Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Business Administration, Engineering, Mathematics, Information Systems or other related field from regionally accredited college/university.
5 years of work experience related to the Duties & Responsibilities of this position, or 2 years as a DBA or Data Engineer.
Demonstrated database programming language such as Python, R or SQL.
Demonstrated database management systems such as Oracle, Microsoft SQL Server, SAP HANA and others.
Cloud data platforms such as Microsoft Azure, Snowflake, Google Cloud Platform, AWS.
Ability to effectively organize own work and teamwork, maintain an extremely high level of accuracy, check own work and the work of others, and keep accurate and auditable records.
Demonstrated interpersonal skills, including collaboration and teamwork.

Primary Duties & Responsibilities

Data and AI Design and Development 

Perform all database-related activities during application system evaluations, development projects, and subsequent implementations. Identify opportunities to reduce data-redundancy and utilize single sources of data with integration of systems through database design and access methods. Coordinate and prioritize work assignments based on business and technical requirements.
Plan and perform database monitoring and tuning activities to ensure optimal performance and response time. Provide consulting and support to systems development team members (and others performing similar functions outside of IT) in efficient SQL and NoSQL coding techniques for optimum database and application performance.
Create, conceptualize, design and maintain data processes, data reliability, data efficiency, data quality, data storage, data models, data performance, data ingestion, all in support of PGS data and AI strategies for data analytics and data visualization.
Plan, coordinate, and implement data models and updates into production on a regularly scheduled basis follow PGS and IT standard SDLC and change management processes and procedures. 

Project and Database Support

Support systems development team members in understanding application data requirements and developing logical database models. Convert logical database designs to physical database models and develop the Data Definition Language (DDL) to create appropriate database objects. Review, test, and make appropriate revisions to vendor-supplied DDL to ensure database integrity.
Design and implement appropriate database backup and recovery strategies based on requirements provided by systems development team members and IT Business Partners. Perform database recovery and restoration as needed.
Participate in technical projects that ensure the ongoing integrity of all corporate-wide databases and database architecture. Perform upgrades to DBMS and data integration products, which include impact analysis, testing, data conversion and implementation.
Create and maintain documentation that supports a historical record of changes made to any data model and display for audit purposes.
Thought Leadership
Maintain knowledge of existing and emerging database technologies and recommends adoption of new technologies where appropriate. Evaluate and recommend database-related products, services, and procedures to enhance productivity and effectiveness. Must maintain sufficient knowledge of the business processes and systems.
Evaluate the data modeling team tasks status and resource utilization and recommend changes to improve the team’s effectiveness.
Develop training and procedure documentation and materials for the data modeling team and facilitate both formal and informal training.

WHAT WILL GIVE YOU A COMPETITIVE EDGE? 

2 years of experience with cloud technologies and platforms such as Microsoft Azure, Amazon Web Services (AWS) or Google Cloud Platform (GCP).
Demonstrated knowledge of utility industry operations and business processes. 
Experience working on data modeling teams requiring expert technical skills and solid understanding of business functions.
Demonstrated competence with cloud storage, cloud tools and Big Data.

WHO WILL BE A PART OF YOUR TEAM? 

Staff augmentation, contractor and/or technical support team.
IT Architects, IT Strategic Planning, Development Staff and Support of various business application towers, Project Management Office, other IT and Business Stakeholders etc.
Consultants (System Integrators, Offshore Teams), Contractors, Customers, Vendors.

HOW YOU’LL CONTRIBUTE? 

Cultivates Innovation and Embraces Change.
Drives Operational Excellence for Customers. 
Speaks Up on Safety, Health, and the Environment. 
Takes Ownership & Acts with Integrity. 
Thinks Strategically & Exercises Sound Judgment. 

WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING WITH US? 

We proudly offer a competitive total rewards package and other perks to help keep you thriving:

Performance Bonus: Earn an annual incentive bonus that recognizes your hard work. 
Comprehensive Health Coverage: Enjoy medical, prescription drug, dental, and vision insurance. 
Retirement Planning: Secure your future with a 401k Retirement Savings Plan and a comprehensive Pension plan. 
Ownership Opportunities: Participate in Emera’s Employee Common Share Purchase Plan and share in our success. 
Time to Recharge: Enjoy Paid Time Off (PTO), paid company holidays, comprehensive mental, financial and physical wellness resources, and paid Parental Leave 
Growth and Development: Take advantage of tuition reimbursement and other training and development opportunities to enhance your skills and career. 
And much more! 

Whether you’re launching your career or looking to advance it, Peoples Gas is the perfect place for you, offering exciting opportunities for a long-term career with a dynamic, growing company. 

Emergency Response Requirements

TECO Energy and its companies serve a role in providing critical services to our community during an emergency. Team members are required to participate in the response/recovery activities related to emergencies/disasters to maintain service to our TECO Energy customers. Team members are required to work in their normal job duties or other assigned activities. Proper compensation will be made in accordance with the company's rules and procedures.

A pre-employment drug screening, as well as a background check (including education, employment, criminal and motor vehicle) will also be conducted as part of the pre-employment screening. A physical may be conducted if required for the role.

Since 1978 Christy's Restaurant has been serving Coral Gables locals and visitors alike. Christy’s provides a classic steakhouse experience from their famous Caesar Salad to the Baked Alaska which is flambeed tableside. The intimate dining rooms with modern artwork set the tone for incredible food and flawless service in the heart of Coral Gables. Ability to stand for long periods of time. Average 20-25 hours per week. Tuesday through Saturday with Sunday and Monday off.

Host / Hostess - Restaurant Responsibilities:

High school diploma or GED

Prior customer service experience in the hospitality industry

Positive attitude and interpersonal skills

Good communication skills and phone etiquette

Flexibility when working alternating shifts

Ability to stand for long periods of time

Host / Hostess - Restaurant Qualifications:

High school diploma or GED

Prior customer service experience in the hospitality industry

Positive attitude and interpersonal skills

Good communication skills and phone etiquette

Flexibility when working alternating shifts

Job description
We are seeking a Registered Behavior Technician (RBT) to provide in-home Applied Behavior Analysis (ABA) therapy services to children with developmental needs, including autism. RBTs work under the supervision of a BCBA to implement individualized treatment plans focused on building communication, social, and daily living skills while reducing challenging behaviors.

This role requires consistency, attention to detail, and the ability to follow structured behavior programs while accurately collecting session data. Candidates should be comfortable working directly with children in a home-based setting and collaborating with families and supervising clinicians.

RBT certification is required or must be actively in progress.

Ideal candidates are dependable, patient, and interested in gaining experience in behavioral health, psychology, education, or related fields.

Job description
Coverview is an AI platform built for Managed Service Providers (MSPs) serving small businesses across the United States. We give MSPs deeper visibility into their operations — what's driving performance, where margin is leaking, and where to focus next.

We're hiring a Founding Growth Intern to support our U.S. market expansion.

What you'll do

Run outbound outreach to MSPs nationwide (qualified leads provided)
Book discovery calls with founders, owners, and key decision-makers
Assist in developing our sales architecture and refining go-to-market messaging
Who we're looking for

Strong interpersonal skills — genuinely comfortable on the phone
Interest in B2B sales, AI, and operational software
Prior sales or outreach experience preferred
Why it's worth your time: You'll work directly with the founder, watch your outreach convert into real pipeline, and gain hands-on exposure to B2B sales execution. Strong performers will be considered for full-time roles.

Passionate about financial freedom? Join our team at to help others manage money and overcome debt. No experience is needed; we provide comprehensive, paid training to ensure your success. Enjoy the flexibility of working from home while building a rewarding career. If you are ready to learn, grow, and make a real impact, apply today to schedule an interview and discuss your future with us.

Job description
Genesis Edge Management

Genesis Edge Management is currently hiring an Entry-Level Account Manager to support our growing client portfolio. This role is ideal for individuals looking to start a career in account management, client relations, and marketing with hands-on training and clear advancement opportunities.

No previous account management experience is required — paid training is provided.

What You’ll Do:

Support and maintain relationships with assigned client accounts

Assist with the execution of marketing and promotional campaigns

Communicate with customers and clients in a professional manner

Track account performance and support daily objectives

Collaborate with internal teams and leadership

Develop foundational skills in account management, communication, and strategy

What We’re Looking For:

Strong communication and interpersonal skills

Positive, coachable, and motivated attitude

Ability to work in a fast-paced, team-oriented environment

Customer service, retail, or restaurant experience is a plus (not required)

Reliable and professional work ethic

What We Offer:

Paid training and ongoing mentorship

Competitive compensation with performance incentives

Clear career growth opportunities into senior account management or leadership

Supportive, team-driven culture

Full-time, consistent schedule

Job description
The Vice President (VP) of FreedomInd.us is a senior executive leader responsible for advancing the organization’s mission, strategic priorities, and operational excellence. The VP works closely with the President/CEO and leadership team to translate vision into execution, strengthen organizational capacity, and ensure sustainable growth and impact.

This role combines strategic leadership, people management, and cross‑functional oversight, serving as a key partner in decision‑making and external representation.

Job description
Founded in Toronto, Real Fruit Bubble Tea has been committed to making fresh fruit drinks healthy and accessible for anyone to enjoy since 2002. We have 137 locations across Canada and USA. As a rapidly expanding business, we want you to join our team and grow with us! There is huge potential for growth, and we welcome anyone who is up to a challenging yet rewarding journey!

 

Position Description

Reporting to the Engineering Manager, the Engineering Officer/Assistant Store Manager will help manage and delegate tasks to the engineering team, contractors, and designers, ensuring that projects are completed on time, within budget, and in compliance with quality standards, codes, and regulations. Additionally, the Engineering Officer/Assistant Store Manager will work in-store on a weekly basis to support operational tasks and ensure smooth coordination between engineering projects and store activities.

 

Expectations of the Successful Candidate

New graduates are welcome!
No previous work experience is required, training will be provided.
Excellent communication skills (written and verbal) and interpersonal skills.
Effective time management, decision-making, and problem-solving skills with a meticulous attention to detail.
Strong ability in working with Microsoft Office suite applications.
Strong multitasking skills and ability to perform tasks effectively under pressure.
High level of organizational capabilities.
Provide strong support to the Store Manager in ensuring smooth operations.
 

Duties and Responsibilities

Assist in daily store operations, including prepping ingredients, making beverages, and maintaining cleanliness.
Support store manager with staff supervision, ensuring smooth operations and excellent customer service.
Manage inventory, stock levels, and order supplies to keep the store running efficiently.
Understand and carry out all tasks assigned by the Engineering Manager.
Collaborate with engineers and other staff to design, develop, test, and execute products and engineering processes.
Ensure all projects and tasks are completed within the allocated budget and timeframe.
Evaluate products and processes, ensuring compliance with quality standards, codes, and regulations.
Perform regular inspections, maintenance, and troubleshooting of equipment.
 

Job Type: Part-time & Full-time

Transportation: This position requires travel; candidates must have access to a reliable vehicle.

About the Role 
Are you a master of logistics, a problem-solver under pressure, and someone who thrives in a fast-moving environment? We’re looking for a highly organized, strategic, and resourceful Operations Coordinator to take charge of scheduling, resource allocation, and workflow optimization for our field operations. 
In this role, you’ll be the heartbeat of our operations, ensuring seamless coordination between teams, tackling logistical challenges, and continuously improving processes to maximize efficiency. When emergencies arise, you’ll be the one making quick, confident decisions to keep everything on track. 
If you love keeping things moving, optimizing operations, creating clarity in uncertainty, and being the central hub of communication, this is the opportunity for you. Join us and play a key role in driving PCS’s success every day! 
 
Key Responsibilities 

Scheduling & Resource Coordination 
 

Develop and maintain daily and long-term schedules dynamic scheduling for field personnel, equipment, and materials across various jobs, optimizing for efficiency, cost-effectiveness, and productivity.  
Ensure that crew members are properly equipped, trained, and clear on their responsibilities before being dispatched daily.  
Develop a deep understanding of job scopes and project requirements to assess how resource allocation impacts job timelines and overall execution. 
Centralized Communication and Conflict Resolution

Act as the primary communication hub, ensuring seamless coordination and job execution among all stakeholders regarding schedules, updates, and shifting priorities.
Serve as the key liaison for field supervisors, project managers, clients, and resource teams to facilitate efficient resource allocation and job execution.
Collaborate closely with field teams, project managers, and clients to optimize job scheduling, proactively identify risks, resolve conflicts, and ensure timely completion of tasks. 
 
Process Improvement & Technology Integration 
 

Leverage software tools (dispatching platforms, GPS tracking, ERP systems) to streamline scheduling, resource management, and real-time job monitoring.
Continuously evaluate and challenge existing dispatching processes, recommending improved solutions to drive operational excellence.
Analyze and track key performance metrics, ensuring compliance with safety, regulatory, and operational standards, and drive solutions to improve performance. 
Managing Operational Compliance 

Work with the safety manager and project manager to ensure that all work is performed in compliance with safety regulations, company policies, and industry standards.
Help and enforce safety protocols for crew members, ensuring that necessary PPE is used, and safety guidelines are followed.
Track crew work hours and attendance, ensuring accurate payroll and compliance with labor regulations 
 

Qualifications 

A bachelor's degree in business administration, operations management, or a STEM field (preferred)
3+ years of experience in logistics coordination, dispatching, or operations (preferred). 
 
Strong problem-solving and critical thinking skills; ability to make real-time decisions while managing multiple moving parts.
Ability to prioritize competing demands and operate efficiently under pressure.
Highly organized and process oriented with a strong knowledge of operational efficiency and execution.
Excellent organizational, multitasking, and time management skills.
Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Outlook, Excel, Word, SharePoint) and quickly learning new software such as ERPs and dispatch systems.
Experience working in environmental services, construction, or field-based operations (preferred)
 

Why Join Us? 
 

Be a part of a fast growing company looking to innovate in the environmental space
Work in a high-impact role where your decisions directly affect operational efficiency
Opportunity to streamline and improve resource allocation operations for a growing company
Be part of a collaborative, problem-solving team that values innovation and strategic thinking

Position Title: R&D Operations Intern

Reports to: R&D Head of Base Business

Location: Hybrid–Remote (Office located in Hackensack, NJ)

Employment Type: Full-time intern (40 hrs per week)

Internship Duration: Summer 2026 (June – August)

Company Profile:

Evermark is a newly formed beauty and personal care company bringing together a portfolio of iconic, everyday brands with enormous reach and untapped potential: Suave, ChapStick, Q-tips, Caress, St. Ives, TIGI Bed Head, Noxzema, Pond’s, Lever 2000. These are brands people know, trust, and use daily - and we are building their next chapter.

Backed by a growth-oriented YellowWood Partners, we operate with speed, focus, and accountability. Our goal is simple: modernize these brands, accelerate growth, and build a company that consistently outperforms expectations. That means sharpening strategy, raising the bar on execution, and making smart, decisive moves across the enterprise - from how we plan and operate to how we go to market.

This is a company in motion. We are scaling quickly, building new capabilities, and creating the operating model as we go. For the right people, that means rare access, real influence, and the opportunity to help shape not just a role, but a company.

 

Position Summary:

The R&D Operations Intern will support the Research & Development Operations team in maintaining accurate, organized, and compliant product and raw material data within Evermark’s SpecRight product lifecycle management (PLM) system.

This internship provides hands-on exposure to the operational backbone of product development within the personal care industry. The intern will collaborate with cross-functional teams and external partners, including raw material suppliers and contract manufacturing organizations (CMOs), to support data accuracy. Additional learning opportunities may include exposure to product manufacturing environments and networking across departments such as R&D, Marketing, and Supply Chain to better understand the innovation lifecycle of personal care products.

This role is ideal for a highly detail-oriented individual interested in gaining hands-on experience in the operational and technical foundation of personal care product lifecycle management.

 

Key Responsibilities:

· Support cleanup and harmonization of raw material and formula records within the SpecRight PLM system

· Follow up with raw material suppliers and/or Contract Manufacturing Organizations (CMOs) on technical documentation, including specifications, safety data sheets, and regulatory information

· Assist in organizing and maintaining technical documentation in alignment with company standards

· Track and document progress

· Support continuous improvement initiatives related to R&D operations and data management

· Participate in cross-functional meetings to gain exposure to portfolio management and product lifecycle processes

· Support additional R&D Operations activities as assigned

Required Qualifications:

· Currently pursuing a degree in Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, Cosmetic Science, Biology, Engineering, or a related technical field

· Strong attention to detail

· Excellent organizational skills and ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously

· Strong written and verbal communication skills

· Comfortable communicating professionally with internal teams and external partners

· Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, including Excel, Outlook, and Lists

· Ability to work both independently and collaboratively in a hybrid work environment

· Demonstrated interest in pursuing a career in Research & Development, Product Development, Regulatory Affairs, or Technical Operations in the personal care, cosmetics, or consumer goods industry

 

Preferred Qualifications:

· Prior internship, laboratory, or academic experience involving data management, technical documentation, or formulation-related coursework

· Familiarity with Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) systems such as SpecRight or similar database platforms

· Coursework or exposure related to cosmetic science, formulation, manufacturing, or regulatory processes

· Experience organizing large datasets or working with structured technical information

 

People Who Thrive Here:

We look for people who take ownership, move with urgency, and care deeply about results.

Across every level and function, success here comes from thinking like an operator; not waiting to be told what to do but seeing what needs to happen and driving it forward. The individual must be comfortable working across teams, influencing without hierarchy, and making progress even when the path isn’t perfectly defined.

This is a fast-moving environment with high standards and real accountability. In return, you’ll have meaningful responsibility, autonomy to lead your work, and the opportunity to grow alongside brands, and a company, that are being actively shaped.

Education, Training, and Experience:

Must be at least 18 years of age
CBT license
RBT Certification
Possess a minimum of a High School Diploma or GED
Other Requirements:

Successful completion of criminal history background check
Negative TB test prior to starting with clients
BLS Certification
Job Duties:

Run skill acquisition programs with clients
Complete notes daily
Collect data and graph data as instructed using Google Sheets/Google Forms
Maintain respect and confidentiality for all clients according to HIPAA and Washington state law.
Maintain and protect the well-being of all clients during treatment sessions
Other job duties as assigned by supervisor
Typical start time:

9 AM
Typical end time:

6 PM
Job Type: Full-time

Pay: $23.00 - $25.00 per hour

Expected hours: 30 – 40 per week

License/Certification:

Certified Behavior Technician License (Required)
Registered Behavior Technician (Required)
Ability to Commute:

Lynnwood, WA
Seattle, WA
 

Work Location: In person

target_role: Wastewater Treatment Plant Operator Grade I
job_description:
Job description
                                          GROW YOU CAREER

Join the City of Roseville Environmental Utilities Department in the role of Wastewater Treatment Plant Operator Grade I

 

Step into a role where you will help power your community by keeping one of the City’s essential systems running smoothly. As a Wastewater Treatment Plant Operator Grade I, you will gain hands-on experience with advanced technology from SCADA systems to energy-producing equipment like microturbines and CNG production systems. This is a great opportunity for someone mechanically inclined who is eager to learn, grow and earn their certification with the support of a knowledgeable team. If you are ready to build a meaningful career in a dynamic, fast-paced environment that values safety, collaboration and innovation, join our team and let’s shape a cleaner, more sustainable future together!

 

The employment list that will be established from this recruitment may be utilized to fill future regular, limited-term and temporary vacancies. The current vacancy is a 1500-hour temporary position.


The City of Roseville is committed to an inclusive and supportive workforce and workplace. We are engaged in understanding the backgrounds and perspectives of our colleagues and those we serve. Our organization is unified in this commitment, because we believe this produces the best results for our organization and community.

 

Examples of Duties

For a complete and detailed job specification and minimum qualifications, click HERE.

Minimum Qualifications

Experience and Training:
Experience:

No experience is required; some experience in repairing/maintaining mechanical equipment and/or knowledge of mechanical processes is preferred.

AND

Training:

Equivalent to completion of the twelfth (12th) grade, GED, or higher level degree.


 

License or Certificate:

Possession of a valid California driver’s license by date of appointment.

Possession of a Wastewater Treatment Plant Operator Grade I certificate issued by the State Water Resources Control Board within one year of date of appointment.

Field Service Engineer (Entry-Level)

Boston, MA (Remote with Regional Travel)

 

Launch Your Career with Probo Medical!

Are you a recent graduate who enjoys hands-on problem solving, technology, and making a real impact? As a Field Service Engineer (FSE), you’ll play a critical role in keeping advanced medical imaging equipment—like ultrasound systems—running safely and efficiently in hospitals and clinics.

This is more than just a technical job—it’s an opportunity to build a meaningful career in healthcare technology. You’ll receive training, mentorship, and real-world experience working directly with cutting-edge equipment and clinical professionals.

 

Why This Role is a Great Start for Your Career

Training & mentorship – No prior field service experience required
Hands-on technical experience with advanced medical imaging technology
Make a real impact by supporting patient care and clinical teams
Independence & variety – no two days are the same
Travel opportunities throughout the Boston region and surrounding states
What You’ll Do

Install, maintain, and repair diagnostic imaging equipment (including ultrasound systems)
Troubleshoot technical issues using electronic tools and problem-solving skills
Perform preventative maintenance and system calibrations
Train healthcare staff on safe and effective equipment use
Partner with clinical teams to ensure equipment is functioning properly
Document service work and maintain accurate records
Manage your schedule and coordinate service visits with customers
Participate in ongoing training to build your technical expertise
What We’re Looking For

Bachelor’s degree (or equivalent) in:
Biomedical Engineering
Electrical Engineering
Electronics Technology
Or a related field
Strong interest in hands-on technical work and problem-solving
Basic understanding of electronics, mechanics, or troubleshooting
Strong communication skills and customer-focused mindset
Willingness to learn, adapt, and work independently
Ability to travel locally (up to 80%)
Bonus if you have:

Internship, co-op, or hands-on technical experience
Exposure to medical devices or imaging equipment
Build a Career That Matters

In this role, you won’t just fix equipment—you’ll help ensure that doctors and clinicians can deliver life-saving care. If you’re ready to apply your degree in a meaningful, fast-paced environment, we want to hear from you.  Apply today!

Taylor Farms is looking for an experienced Ranch Hand to join their diverse, fast paced family operation in Goodland, Kansas. The Ranch Hand must have a strong work ethic, be detail orientated and have 3-5 years of cattle experience.

Ranch Hand Compensation & Benefits:

·      $48,000-$65,000 per year, based on experience

·      Year-end bonus

·      Employer provides health insurance for the employee

·      Paid time off

·      Work pickup

·      Beef

Ranch Hand Job Duties:

·      Conduct routine livestock inspections on all cattle.

·      Handle cattle with low stress.

·      Feed, water and put out salt and minerals

·      Assist with artificial insemination process

·      Assist with calving

·      Identify and treat sick cattle

·      Process and work cattle regularly

·      Rotate cattle on pastures and corn stalks

·      Install and fix fence

·      Ability to monitor cattle health via side‑by‑side vehicles and/or horseback riding

·      Safely operate a semi-truck during harvest

·      Perform general maintenance and repairs on equipment

Ranch Hand Schedule: Hours vary based on season

·      Monday – Friday, 7:00 AM – 6:00 PM, rotating weekends for chores

The Ranch Hand must have an acceptable driving record as reflected by MVR. A class A Farm CDL is required, or the willingness to get one.

Reward yourself with a New Career at Ovation Rehabilitation!   

 

We have a Part-time Occupational Therapist (OTR) opportunity available at our location in Austintown, Ohio.  This position offers a flexible schedule. 

 

Active state license as an Occupational Therapist is required.  

 

With many years of successful industry experience behind us on a national level, Ovation Rehabilitation has entered the contract rehabilitation industry with a fresh vision and superior culture that recognizes, appreciates, and rewards individual and team contributions.    

  

With the focus on those that we care for, Ovation Rehabilitation is a company that you will CHEER for and APPLAUD!!   

 

Come join our team as we travel into the future and be a part of the growth and excitement. Enjoy what you do and grow with a company that you can be proud of.  

 

When you join our team, we offer the opportunity for a rewarding career, competitive benefits and the potential for career advancement. In addition to our open and supportive culture that is second to none and features our “Round of Applause” employee recognition program as well as individual recognition for YOUR birthday and other personal events we offer dedicated facility-based teams with on-site management and experienced regional management support that is locally based. Our “Clap -Out” events recognize and celebrate resident successes.    

 

Our front-loaded PTO program is immediately available for you to use, with no black-out or waiting period.  

 

As a Full-Time OTR you will also have the ability to choose:  

 

401(k) – Available on Benefits Eligible Day One with a company match 
Paid Time Off – Available Immediately  
Life Insurance 
Short Term and Long Term Disability  
Advancement Opportunities 
Employee Referral Bonus Program   
  

Whether you are looking now or will be in the future, we invite you check out our career page. Go to Careers - OVATION Rehabilitation  
 
Ovation Rehabilitation is an equal opportunity employer.

Job description:

About Us Founded by twin brothers David and Daniel Michelsen, Twin Buds Reconstruction is a specialized construction company serving the Austin and San Antonio areas. We exclusively focus on the build-back phase following water, fire, and mold losses, primarily serving the commercial sector. We pride ourselves on our uncompromising quality, high attention to detail, and a commitment to complete customer satisfaction on every project.

Role Overview We are looking for a highly organized and detail-oriented Project Manager to join our team. The Project Manager is responsible for taking a project from assignment through closeout while maintaining strict control over scheduling, subcontractors, budgets, and quality. You will not be responsible for estimating; your focus will be entirely on executing the provided scope on time, maximizing profitability, and delivering a flawless final product.

Core Responsibilities

Project Ownership: Take full command of commercial reconstruction projects following the estimator hand-off, ensuring a seamless transition from approved scope to active execution.
Site & Quality Management: Drive daily progress through active, on-the-ground management. Visually verify the quality of all trade work to ensure it meets our strict, high-detail standards before approving next steps.
Subcontractor Coordination: Source, schedule, and manage skilled trades, ensuring strict adherence to timelines, safety expectations, and required vendor compliance.
Financial Oversight: Protect project profitability by managing the execution budget, meticulously tracking expenses, and preventing material or labor waste.
Client Communication: Serve as the professional face of the company on-site, providing proactive daily updates to client contacts and maintaining high customer satisfaction.
System Tracking: Leverage Monday.com and Google Sheets to maintain accurate, real-time project schedules, coordinate internal workflows, and track milestones.
Closeouts: Drive projects across the finish line by managing aggressive punch lists, conducting final walkthroughs, and overseeing comprehensive project closeouts.
Requirements & Qualifications

Experience: 1 to 2 years of construction or project management experience is preferred.
Tech-Savvy: Working knowledge of Monday.com and Google Sheets is highly preferred for daily tracking and updates.
Critical Thinking & Problem Solving: Ability to anticipate on-site challenges, troubleshoot schedule conflicts (like material delays), and pivot quickly to keep projects moving forward.
Advanced Communication: Must be able to have firm, professional conversations with subcontractors to hold them accountable, while simultaneously delivering polished, reassuring updates to our commercial clients.
Time Management & Prioritization: Highly organized and capable of managing multiple active job sites, efficiently planning daily routes, and prioritizing tasks to meet strict deadlines.
Attention to Detail: An uncompromising eye for quality control, ensuring nothing slips through the cracks, especially during the final punch list phase.
Logistics: Ability to travel locally to visit job sites daily, verify trade progress, and occasionally transport materials. A personal truck is preferred, but not mandatory.
Compensation

Total Compensation: Includes a competitive base salary plus a performance-based commission structure tied directly to execution speed, project profitability, and quality standards.
Benefits:

Fuel card
Paid time off
Professional development assistance

Accountant - on-site job

Salary Range: $60,000 - $63,000 - equivalent to $30.77 to $ 32.31 based on a 37.5 hrs. weekly work schedule

 

We are seeking a dedicated and detail-oriented Accountant to join our team. The Accountant will play a crucial role in ensuring the financial health and accountability of our organization. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in accounting principles, excellent analytical skills, and a passion for supporting the mission of El Nido Family Centers.

Overview

Under the direction of the Controller Assistant and Controller, the Accountant is responsible for ensuring accurate and timely fiscal reporting in compliance with Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) and applicable federal, state, and program regulations. The Accountant prepares financial information for the agency’s programs and departments, as well as has responsibilities with ensuring timely monthly and annual closing processes. Responsible for processing accounts receivable, maintaining general ledger, and preparation of various reports

Job Responsibilities

Prepares and submits billing invoices to funding sources in compliance with all contract terms
Communicates directly with third parties to answer questions, resolve issues related to billing, and responds to inquiries as needed
Prepares or assists with preparation of program budgets and budget modifications
Reconciles G/L account and bank account statements
Monitor contract expenditures to ensure alignment with billing agreements and allowable costs guidelines
Report any inconsistencies related to the approved budget to Controller Assistant/ Controller and modify budget if needed
Ensure all transactions for programs including accounts payable and general ledger entries are allowable in accordance with the funder’s criteria and are recorded accurately
Maintains state of audit readiness for billing and expense records, gathers data for financial audits and other grantor site visits. Supports Controller with all external audits.
Answers accounting procedure questions by researching and interpreting accounting policy and regulations.
Prepares special financial reports by collecting, analyzing, and summarizing account information and trends.
Maintains accounting files including appropriate spreadsheets for cost center allocations.
Ensures compliance with internal policies, State, and Federal regulatory requirements and professional standards.
Monitors, reviews, and verifies accounting records and transactions for accuracy, timeliness, and adequacy of supporting documentation
Assists with reviewing and processing Accounts Payable as needed basis
Qualifications

B.A. degree in accounting, finance or related field from an accredited university.
2+ years of experience in accounting receivable and maintenance of routine accounting records.
Proficiency in accounting software. Financial Edge preferred
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite. Advanced skills in Excel.
Knowledge of and experience with nonprofit accounting principles.
Knowledge and understanding of financial statements and budgeting forecasts.
Strong analytical problem-solving and decision-making skills.
Ability to exercise effective communication skills and to explain financial information
Flexible, detail-oriented, self-starter with the ability to work effectively in a fast-paced environment.
Ability to work with independently with limited supervisor.
Good team player skills
Ability to maintain discretion when handling sensitive financial information.

About the job
About SuperSonic POS



SuperSonic POS is a modern point-of-sale and retail technology platform based in Tampa, FL.



We power thousands of retail locations across convenience, grocery, fuel, and specialty retail with:



Cloud-based POS and back office
Real-time reporting and analytics
Inventory and pricebook management
Integrated payments and value-added services


We’re a lean, product-focused engineering team building systems that handle high transaction volumes and real-world retail complexity.



Role: Full Stack Software Engineer (Hybrid – Tampa, FL)
This is a full-time, hybrid role with 2 days per week in-office at our Westshore Tampa location. The first 90 days are fully on-site for faster onboarding & integration to the team.


You’ll work across the stack — from our web and mobile front-ends to backend services — to ship features that directly impact thousands of stores.


What you’ll do


Build and maintain full-stack features in our POS and back-office applications
Work across modern TypeScript, including both frontend and backend services
Develop and consume APIs (REST and/or GraphQL)
Work with relational databases and caching systems
Contribute to system design for performance, reliability, and scalability
Collaborate with product and engineering to define and deliver new functionality
Participate in code reviews and help improve engineering standards and patterns
Requirements
4+ years of professional software engineering experience
Strong proficiency with TypeScript
Experience with at least one modern frontend framework (e.g., React)
Experience building backend services (e.g., Node.js or similar)
Comfortable working with relational databases (e.g., PostgreSQL, MySQL, etc.)
Familiarity with APIs (REST and/or GraphQL)
Experience with containers (e.g., Docker) and CI/CD
Strong debugging, problem-solving, and ownership mindset
Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced, collaborative environment
Nice-to-Have
Experience in fintech, payments, or POS/retail systems
Experience with in-store hardware integrations (printers, scanners, scales, etc.)
Exposure to cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure)
Familiarity with event-driven systems, queues, or streaming
Contributions to open source or a strong GitHub/portfolio


Compensation & Benefits
Base salary: $100,000–$150,000 (Tampa – Hybrid, 2x/week on-site)
Exact offer depends on experience, seniority, and interview performance.
Health, dental, and vision insurance
401(k) with company match
Two weeks paid time off + eight paid holidays
Flexible work hours with a hybrid schedule
Strong ownership, visibility, and impact in a growing company

About the job
Forward Deployed Engineer (AI / Data / Systems)



Overview

We’re hiring Forward Deployed Engineers to build and deploy AI systems in real-world environments.


This is a client-embedded engineering role. You will take ambiguous problems, design technical solutions, and ship production systems that integrate LLMs, data infrastructure, and operational workflows. You are not building demos—you are building systems that are used, measured, and iterated on.


What You’ll Do

Build Production AI Systems

Design and deploy LLM-powered applications using APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, open-source models)
Implement retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipelines using vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, FAISS)
Build agent-based systems using orchestration frameworks (LangChain, LangGraph, custom agents)
Develop evaluation pipelines for non-deterministic outputs (prompt testing, scoring, guardrails)
Data & Systems Engineering

Build and optimize data pipelines (batch + streaming) using tools like Airflow, Kafka, or similar
Design data models and integrate structured/unstructured data sources (APIs, warehouses, document stores)
Work with data platforms such as Snowflake, Databricks, BigQuery, or Postgres
Implement caching, indexing, and retrieval strategies for performance and cost optimization
Backend & Infrastructure

Build scalable backend services and APIs (Python, FastAPI, Node.js, TypeScript)
Deploy services using Docker, Kubernetes, and cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure)
Implement CI/CD pipelines and versioning for models, prompts, and workflows
Manage secrets, auth, and secure system integrations in production environments
Agentic Workflows & Automation

Design multi-step agent workflows with tool use, memory, and state management
Implement function calling, tool routing, and structured output handling
Build systems that combine LLM reasoning with deterministic business logic
Client-Embedded Execution

Work directly with stakeholders to translate business problems into technical systems
Iterate rapidly in live environments with real users and feedback loops
Own delivery from prototype → production → optimization


What You Bring

Engineering Depth

3–8+ years of software engineering experience
Strong proficiency in Python and/or TypeScript
Experience building distributed systems, APIs, and data-intensive applications
AI / LLM Experience

Hands-on experience with:
Prompt engineering and evaluation
RAG architectures and vector search
LLM orchestration frameworks (LangChain, LangGraph, or similar)
Understanding of model limitations (hallucinations, latency, cost tradeoffs, context windows)
Data & Infrastructure

Experience with databases (SQL + NoSQL), data pipelines, and ETL processes
Familiarity with cloud infrastructure and containerized deployments
Understanding of system performance, observability, and scaling
Builder Mindset

You operate well in ambiguity and move quickly from idea → implementation
You debug systems end-to-end (not just your layer)
You care about whether the system actually works in production
Communication

Able to work directly with non-technical stakeholders
Can explain system design, tradeoffs, and limitations clearly
Comfortable operating in client-facing environments


What Sets You Apart

Experience deploying AI systems in production environments at scale
Built agent-based systems or internal AI tooling used by real users
Experience with:
Embedding models and semantic search
Evaluation frameworks (human-in-the-loop, automated scoring)
Prompt/version management systems
Background in startups, consulting, or high-ownership environments


Why This Role

You’ll build systems that are actually used—not prototypes
You’ll work across the full stack of modern AI systems
You’ll have ownership over real outcomes, not just tickets
You’ll operate at the edge of applied AI in production environments

About the job
Position: Python Software Developer

Contract Duration: 12+ Months

Location: St. Petersburg, FL (Hybrid, 3 days onsite)



Duties:

We are seeking a talented and passionate Python Developer to join our team and contribute to the development and implementation of innovative Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems. You will play a crucial role in designing, building, and maintaining applications that leverage the power of large language models (LLMs) and advanced retrieval techniques to create cutting-edge AI solutions.



Skills:

Strong Python programming skills:

Demonstrated proficiency in Python programming, including experience with relevant libraries and frameworks (e.g., FastAPI, Flask, Pandas, NumPy).

Experience with LLMs and RAG systems:

Familiarity with large language models and retrieval-augmented generation techniques, including experience with LLM APIs and retrieval systems.

Experience with data retrieval and indexing:

Experience with data retrieval from various sources (e.g., databases, APIs, file systems) and building and managing retrieval indices.

Knowledge of data structures and algorithms:

Understanding of fundamental data structures and algorithms relevant to building efficient and scalable RAG applications.

Experience with cloud computing platforms (e.g., AWS, GCP, Azure):

Familiarity with cloud computing platforms and their services for deploying and scaling RAG applications.

Strong problem-solving and analytical skills:

Ability to identify, analyze, and solve complex problems related to data retrieval, LLM integration, and RAG system optimization.

Bonus Points:

Experience with specific LLM frameworks (e.g., LangChain, Hugging Face Transformers).

Familiarity with search engines and information retrieval techniques.

Experience with machine learning and deep learning concepts.

Experience with building and deploying production-ready applications.

Contribution to open-source projects related to RAG or LLMs.



Education:

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related technical field required

Master's degree in Computer Science, AI/ML, or related field preferred

Relevant professional certifications in Python, cloud platforms, or AI/ML technologies are a plus
